steps.title: Check current clickhouse setup
steps.description:
Verify your clickhouse version and configuration:
```bash
clickhouse-client --version
```
This feature requires a working clickhouse installation.

steps.title: Review clickhouse configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant clickhouse configuration:
I want use the follow sql query, but it does work, in MySQL it is ok.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Correlated subqueries
steps.description:
, thank you for pointing that out!

I see that this feature is not currently supported in both the old and new analyzers (`allow_experimental_analyzer = 1`). 

It's a fundamental feature and will require some development effort. However, given its relevance to SQL compatibility, we are encouraged to implement it.

I've discussed this with  and , and we have decided to start working on it after the new analyzer is ready (tracking issue:

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "Correlated subqueries" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
, thank you for pointing that out!

I see that this feature is not currently supported in both the old and new analyzers (`allow_experimental_analyzer = 1`). 

It's a fundamental feature and will require some development effort. However, given its relevance to SQL compatibility, we are encouraged to implement it.

prerequisites.description: A working clickhouse installation or development environment.
